clusters.topic: Students use AI everywhere but don't trust it, and worry it's killing their job prospects
clusters.summary: Multiple large surveys (CSU system, UC system, San Diego regional) show AI use among students is now near-universal, but trust is low and fear about AI replacing entry-level jobs is high.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Students openly admit heavy AI reliance while simultaneously distrusting AI output and fearing it devalues their future job prospects.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: CSU survey (94,000 respondents): 95% use AI, 84% ChatGPT, 82% job-security worry. San Diego survey: about 90% use ChatGPT, 80% express multiple concerns. UC-affiliated study: about two-thirds use GenAI, 9%+ admit cheating, access disparities by income.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Myth vs Reality: 'AI helps me study' vs '82% of us are scared it kills our job chances'

clusters.topic: Class of 2026 entry-level job market: hiring barely up, AI cutting into the bottom rung
clusters.summary: NACE/CNBC/Entrepreneur reporting shows hiring growth for the Class of 2026 is marginal at best, entry-level postings are down year over year, and AI is cited as a factor shrinking junior roles at major tech employers.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Soon-to-graduate students describe applying to 100+ jobs without a single interview.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: NACE: only 5.6% hiring growth projected, 45% of employers rate market 'fair' (worst since 2020-21). CNBC: entry-level postings down 6-7% YoY, AI linked to 25% drop in recent-grad hiring at Meta/Microsoft/Google. Entrepreneur: anecdotes of 100+ applications with zero interviews.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Reality check: what the Class of 2026 job hunt actually looks like, in numbers

clusters.topic: Student loan system overhaul July 1, plus record default numbers
clusters.summary: Federal loan repayment plans are being eliminated/restructured starting July 1, 2026 (SAVE plan gone, Grad PLUS ended, Parent PLUS capped), arriving at the same time defaults hit a record 9.16 million.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Borrowers facing fewer repayment options just as collections resume after the pandemic pause.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: Bloomberg: 9.16M in default (record), 20% of 43M federal borrowers 1+ year behind. CBS/NPR: July 1 policy overhaul eliminates several repayment plans and ends Grad PLUS loans.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Checklist: what changes for your student loan on July 1

clusters.topic: Canada tightens the door on international students while easing work rules for those already in
clusters.summary: Canada's 2026 study permit cap keeps new international intake well below pre-cap levels even as StatCan shows enrolment back to pandemic lows, while a separate April 2026 rule change removes the co-op work permit requirement for students already enrolled.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: International applicants face a much narrower door in, while current students get one practical paperwork barrier removed.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: IRCC: 408,000 permits in 2026 (155,000 new, 253,000 extensions); grad/PhD exempt from provincial attestation as of Jan 1, 2026. Global News/StatCan: enrolment down to pandemic levels. CIC News: co-op work permit no longer required as of April 1, 2026.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Before/After: what changed for international students in Canada in 2026

clusters.topic: Side hustles have shifted from a trend to a financial necessity for many students
clusters.summary: Community-college student journalism frames side hustles as something most students now need to cover costs, set against national gig-economy participation data for Gen Z.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Students describe juggling part-time work alongside coursework as the norm, not the exception.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: Single campus-news source describing the shift; framed as first-person/op-ed style reporting rather than a large-scale survey.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Reality check: how many of us are actually working a side hustle just to get by

clusters.topic: Rising tuition meets a widening pay gap between majors
clusters.summary: Tuition keeps climbing (about 2.3% at four-year schools for 2026-27) while the lifetime earnings gap between the highest- and lowest-paying majors is roughly $1.9 million, sharpening the stakes of major choice.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: EducationData.org: 2026-27 tuition projected up ~3.25% overall, ~2.3% at 4-year schools. Research.com: chemical/computer/aerospace engineering top median salaries; ~$1.9M lifetime gap between highest and lowest paying majors.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Comparison: what your major is actually projected to cost vs. pay back

clusters.topic: Loneliness and burnout statistics behind viral 'bed rotting' coping habits
clusters.summary: Research from Inside Higher Ed and a Gallup-sourced burnout piece quantify how widespread loneliness and burnout are among college-age students, tied to a viral low-effort coping behavior ('bed rotting').
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Students describe 'bed rotting' (extended unstructured time in bed) as a common, normalized stress response.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: Inside Higher Ed: 60%+ of under-25 students feel lonely sometimes/always, 70%+ among LGBTQ+ students. The Source/Gallup-sourced: Gen Z sleeps least of any generation at the same age; 55% try social-media-driven stress habits, 24% specifically 'bed rot'.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Data check: how common is loneliness/burnout really, and what's the most common (unhealthy) coping habit

excluded_items.reason: Roommate horror-story roundup includes an anecdote involving an alleged allergic-reaction hospitalization tied to an identifiable-enough situation; pre-filter risk on individual incident framing. Not formed into its own cluster this cycle to avoid sensationalizing a specific case; could be revisited later as a purely structural 'roommate red flag checklist' piece with no incident-specific details.
